name: Peñacorada Perimeter Trail
name_original: Perimetral al Peñacorda – vuelta desde Cistierna
description: This difficult 26.5-mile mountain biking loop near Cistierna encircles the Peñacorada massif, offering diverse forests and panoramic views.
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Cistierna traverse a landscape shaped by the Picos de Europa foothills and historical mining activities. The region features varied terrain, from river valleys to elevated mountain passes, with significant elevation changes. Riders can expect to encounter a mix of forest paths, open tracks, and routes that explore the area's industrial heritage. Best mountain bike trails around Cistierna The most popular mountain bike trail is Peñacorada Perimeter Trail, a 26.5 miles (43 km) difficult trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route offers expansive views around the Peñacorada massif. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Coal Mining Basins Route, a difficult 27.2 miles (44 km) path. This trail explores the historical mining areas, providing a unique blend of natural and industrial scenery. Local mountain bikers also love the Abandoned Mines – View of Peña Castiello loop from Cistierna, a 23.0 miles (37 km) trail leading through former mining landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 27 minutes. Mountain biking around Cistierna is defined by mountainous terrain, river valleys, and historical mining areas. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy paths to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Cistierna are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.5 stars from a few reviews. More than 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cistierna's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cistierna?
A: There are over 20 mountain bike trails around Cistierna, offering a diverse range of experiences from river valleys to elevated mountain passes. The region's landscape is shaped by the Picos de Europa foothills and historical mining activities.

Q: Are there easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Cistierna?
A: Yes, Cistierna offers some routes suitable for beginners. There is at least one easy mountain bike trail, and several moderate options that can be a good starting point for those looking to build their skills.

Q: What kind of challenging routes are available for experienced mountain bikers?
A: Experienced mountain bikers will find plenty of challenging routes, with 11 difficult trails featuring significant elevation changes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036137206/penacorada-perimeter-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñacorada Perimeter Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a 43 km route with over 1000 meters of elevation gain, offering expansive views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20501181" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Mines – Mina Imponderable loop from Cistierna&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 71 km with more than 1600 meters of ascent.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Cistierna?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Cistierna are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. This includes popular trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20500986" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Abandoned Mines – View of Peña Castiello loop from Cistierna&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the best times of year for mountain biking in Cistierna?
A: The best times for mountain biking in Cistierna are typically spring and autumn, when the weather is milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can also be good, but be prepared for warmer temperatures, especially at lower elevations. Winter riding is possible, but some higher elevation trails may be affected by snow or ice, requiring appropriate gear and caution.

Q: What kind of views and natural attractions can I expect along the trails?
A: The trails offer diverse views, from river valleys to the foothills of the Picos de Europa. You can expect stunning vistas such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6308896" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Riaño Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and panoramic outlooks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5553308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pico Gilbo, Pico Yordas, and Riaño Reservoir from Mirador Las Breñas Dondes Macho&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features unique geological formations and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3428812" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;León Fjords&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or landmarks to see while mountain biking?
A: Yes, Cistierna's landscape is rich in history, particularly its mining heritage. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036104179/coal-mining-basins-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coal Mining Basins Route&lt;/a&gt; explore former industrial areas. You can also encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5475673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente del Mercadillo&lt;/a&gt; and the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4704854" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mina Imponderable&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are the typical trail conditions like around Cistierna?
A: Trail conditions vary depending on the route and recent weather. You'll encounter a mix of forest paths, open tracks, and gravel roads. Given the mountainous terrain, some sections can be rocky or steep. It's always advisable to check local weather forecasts before heading out, especially after heavy rain, which can make some trails muddy or slippery.

Q: Where can I find parking for mountain bike trails in Cistierna?
A: While specific parking areas are not detailed for every trail, Cistierna and its surrounding villages typically offer public parking options. It's recommended to look for designated parking in town centers or near trailheads, especially for longer routes. Always ensure you park responsibly and respect local regulations.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Cistierna?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cistierna are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.5 stars from several reviews. Over 300 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique blend of natural beauty and historical industrial landscapes.

Q: Are there any less-known or 'hidden gem' mountain bike routes in the area?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' are often discovered by local exploration, routes that delve into the historical mining areas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036104179/coal-mining-basins-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coal Mining Basins Route&lt;/a&gt;, offer a unique experience away from purely natural landscapes, providing a different kind of adventure and historical insight.
tips:
The Mercadillo Bridge is a historic civil structure located over the Esla River, near Sorriba del Esla, within the municipality of Cistierna (province of León).
It likely has Roman origins, forming part of the ancient Esla road or Via Saliámica.
It is mentioned in medieval documents, including records from the Monastery of Gradefes and the will of Countess Doña Sancha in 1234, confirming its existence and use during the Middle Ages.
Near the town of Fuente de Peñacorda, we find this beautiful spot. An ideal place for mountain bike lovers who will enjoy a nice ''single track''
Rolling along a wide forest track, we find these beautiful views of the town of Fuentes de Peñacorada.

faqs.question: Is the Perimetral al Peñacorda – vuelta desde Cistierna trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is generally categorized as a difficult mountain bike ride by Komoot, and an 'intermediate race route' with 'quite technical' sections in local descriptions. It features substantial elevation changes and varied terrain, making it more suitable for experienced riders.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a varied and spectacular landscape, with a mix of tracks and trails, including both technical sections and smoother paths. You'll ride through diverse forests of pines, oaks, and beeches, and cross green meadows. Be aware that some areas might be muddy, rutted, or overgrown, especially after rains. You might even encounter remnants of ancient Roman roads, known as 'Huella de las Legiones'.

faqs.question: What notable sights and viewpoints can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail encircles the impressive Peñacorada massif, offering stunning panoramic views throughout. You can expect distant glimpses of peaks like Pico Peñacorada, Picos de Mampodre, and even the Picos de Europa from higher vantage points. Specific highlights include the Puente del Mercadillo, El Espinadal, and the View of Fuentes de Peñacorada.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: While not explicitly stated, given the potential for mud and overgrown vegetation, it's advisable to ride during drier seasons, typically late spring, summer, and early autumn. This will ensure better trail conditions and clearer views. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Perimetral al Peñacorda – vuelta desde Cistierna trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Cistierna. You should be able to find suitable parking within the town, which serves as the primary access point for this loop route. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but Cistierna is the designated starting point.

faqs.question: Is this mountain biking route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether dogs are allowed or if there are specific leash rules for this trail. It's always best to check with local authorities or visitor centers in Cistierna before bringing your dog to ensure compliance with any regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to ride this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no mention of permits or entrance fees required for riding the Perimetral al Peñacorda – vuelta desde Cistierna trail. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with several other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Camino Olvidado (Puente Almuhey-La Robla), La Huella de las Legiones, Ruta de las Cuencas Mineras del Carbón, and the Camino Vadiniense.

faqs.question: Are e-bikes allowed on the Perimetral al Peñacorda route?
faqs.answer: Yes, e-bikes are permitted on this route, allowing more riders to enjoy the challenging terrain and stunning scenery of the Peñacorada massif.

faqs.question: What kind of forests and vegetation will I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through diverse forests, primarily featuring pines, oaks, and beeches. You'll also pass through lush green meadows, especially on the northern face of the Peñacorada massif, which is known for its varied and rich vegetation.

faqs.question: Is there a local club that promotes or manages information about this route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the local 'Club de Montaña Los Rejos' actively promotes and manages information about the Perimetral al Peñacorda route. They even organize a 'Pequevuelta Peñacorada' for younger riders, indicating a strong local connection to the trail.
